mapping.a_element: residents on a square lattice treated as Ising spins
mapping.b_element: economic agents treated as interacting spins

mapping.a_element: a 'temperature' T controlling stochasticity of moves

brief_justification: Both merely invoke the broad program of applying spin models from statistical physics to social/economic systems, but the specific machinery differs: A is a concrete zero-temperature Ising ferromagnet whose neighbour couplings produce ordering (segregation) plus a self-organised temperature, whereas B is a methodological essay advocating spin-glass physics — quenched disorder and frustration yielding metastable states, aging and crises — and supplies no concrete governing model to map role-for-role. Ferromagnetic ordering versus spin-glass frustration are distinct mechanisms, so the overlap is generic shared vocabulary ('spins', 'temperature') rather than the same transferable machinery.
